summary: sensivity ships Windows persistence + obfuscated payload + HWID exfil masquerading as a Microsoft OneDrive component
details: On `require('sensivity')` (main = launcher.js), the package silently installs a Windows auto-start under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run with the value name 'OneDriveUpdate', pointing wscript.exe at a bundled VBS file 'OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs' that re-launches `node launcher.js` hidden (window style 0). This impersonates Microsoft's OneDrive updater to evade casual inspection and gives the package permanent persistence on every Windows login with no user prompt. launcher.js then reads server.obf.js — a heavily-obfuscated (obfuscator.io string-array + RC4-decoded literals) ~85KB bundle — and passes it to `eval`, hiding the runtime behavior from review. Inside that bundle, generateHwid() reads HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via powershell, os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial via WMIC, SHA-256s them, and POSTs the resulting hardware fingerprint plus a license key to a hardcoded remote endpoint. The package additionally sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` to impersonate a legitimate Windows OS process in Task Manager, and gates its UI behind a powershell scan of Chrome window titles for 'YouTube' (streamer-evasion). Despite a package.json description of 'Sensivity - Mobile Control Panel', the bundled native module sens.node (6.5MB Windows PE) and the public/ UI expose Aimbot, Triggerbot, ESP/Visuals, Radar, Vehicle Spawn, and Silent panels — the package is a game cheat shipping with hostile evasion and persistence machinery, not a control-panel utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
